name: Harness Alpha Tier Policy Rulebook
purpose: Reference used by the Policy Critic to choose both the ideal objective proof tier and the current operational acceptance tier for a generated environment.
core_principles: Compare prompts by physical intent, object roles, and success predicate rather than exact word overlap.
The generated Architect may propose a minimum tier, but the Policy Critic is the independent reviewer.
Finite direct physical objectives should require solved verification when a generic validator can execute their subgoals.
Open-ended, adversarial, strategy-heavy, or long-horizon game tasks may accept progress verification until stronger solvers exist.
Never lower the objective tier just because the current rollout controller is weak.
The objective_tier records what proof the task deserves in principle.
The operational_acceptance_tier records what the current harness should enforce today.

Operational acceptance may be lower than the objective tier only when the task family is open-ended, long-horizon, adversarial, or not yet covered by a solved validator; record the capability_gap explicitly.

tier_guidance.5: Require check_objective() to become true in headless validation. Use for finite reach, touch, collect, push/place, short survival, and explicit ordered subgoal tasks.
tier_guidance.4: Require measurable deterministic progress. Use for open-ended games, adversarial tasks, long-horizon strategy, complex balance/control, or novel mechanisms not yet covered by a solved validator.
tier_guidance.3: Only acceptable for exploratory worlds without a concrete objective; should be rare.
tier_guidance.0: Reject malformed code, missing check_objective, invalid profiles, or unsafe capability shortcuts.

veto_rules: If executable generic subgoals include agent_reach_region, agent_touch_object, or move_object_to_region, operational_acceptance_tier must be at least Tier 5.
If the prompt asks for a finite object placement or finite target collection objective, operational_acceptance_tier must be Tier 5.
If check_objective is missing or the objective profile is malformed, reject regardless of task family.
If the critic output is malformed, fall back to the generated profile plus deterministic guardrails.
